summary:
The process described involves an irreversible change that results in a material gaining strength when heated, fitting the classical description of vulcanization where rubber-like substances become significantly tougher under thermal processing; zinc oxide-eugenol cement exemplifies such behavior by transforming into a hardened form post-mixing/curing which cannot revert back fully like temporary physical changes observed elsewhere.
The answer is option D because it describes a permanent alteration leading to increased durability akin to how natural latex becomes stronger during industrial vulcanizing treatments rather than just Vulcanization is characterized by an irreversible chemical transformation that enhances the mechanical properties of polymers, particularly橡胶材料，在加热和化学交联的作用下变得更强韧且永久改变其结构；锌氧化物-丁香油水泥在混合并固化后会经历这种不可逆的变化过程，并形成一种
